Born: 2nd September 1243 at Christchurch, Hampshire
Earl of Gloucester
Earl of Hertford
Died: 7th December 1295 at Monmouth Castle, Monmouthshire
Gilbert was nicknamed the 'Red Earl' after the colour …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester於1243年9月2日出生在英國哈普爾可利斯丘其時，他的父親Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester21歲，母親Maud de Lacy20歲。他於1253年與Alice de Lusignan結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1295年12月7日在威爾士蒙姆爾蒙姆斯 卡素享年52歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester was born on 2 September 1243, in Christchurch, Hampshire, England, his father, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, was 21 and his mother, Maud de Lacy, was 20. He married Alice de Lusignan in 1253. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 7 December 1295, in Monmouth Castle, Monmouthshire, Wales, at the age of 52, and was buried in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester est né le 2 septembre 1243, à Christchurch, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Maud de Lacy, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Lusignan en 1253. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 7 décembre 1295, à Monmouth Castle, Monmouthshire, Galles, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester am 2. September 1243 in Christchurch, Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester 21 und seine Mutter Maud de Lacy 20. Er hat 1253 Alice de Lusignan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 7. Dezember 1295 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Monmouth Castle, Monmouthshire, Wales und wurde in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester è nato il il 2 settembre 1243 nel Christchurch, Hampshire, England, il suo padre, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Maud de Lacy, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Alice de Lusignan nel 1253. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 7 dicembre 1295, nel Monmouth Castle, Monmouthshire, Galles, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucesterが1243年9月2日にイギリスハンプシュアイアチリストチャーチで生まれた時，父Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucesterは21歳，母Maud de Lacyは20歳であった。1253年にAlice de Lusignanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1295年12月7日にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアモンモウス カストルで52歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーテウケスブリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester(이)가 1243년 9월 2일 영국 함프실르 크리스트c후르치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Lacy(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1253년 Alice de Lusignan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1295년 12월 7일 웨일스 몬모우스실르 몬모우스 카스틀에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester nasceu em 2 de setembro de 1243, em Christchurch, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Lacy, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Lusignan em 1253. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 7 de dezembro de 1295, em Monmouth Castle, Monmouthshire, Gales, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.Westminster Abbey, London, England: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester родился 2 сентября 1243 г. в местности крисчурч, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Maud de Lacy, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Lusignan в 1253 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 7 декабря 1295 г. в местности монмоут кастл, монмоутшир, Уэльс в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester nació el 2 de septiembre de 1243, en Christchurch, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Maud de Lacy, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Alice de Lusignan en 1253. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 7 de diciembre de 1295, en Monmouth Castle, Monmouthshire, Gales, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
